mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series 328i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 3 Series 328i engine costs £2,200–£5,000 supply only, varying by generation: M52 £1,800–£3,000, M52TU £2,200–£3,800, N52 £2,500–£4,500, N20 £2,800–£5,500. Fitting adds £900–£1,500. Check the Era Map above for your engine code.

That depends on which engine your 328i has. The M52 and M52TU sixes are robust but cooling system is the achilles heel. The N52 magnesium six is refined but suffers oil leaks. The N20 turbo is the most powerful but has a well-documented timing chain guide failure risk pre-2015. The E46 M52TU is considered the sweet spot for naturally aspirated reliability.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series 328i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20: timing chain guide failure (cold-start rattle, £1,500–£3,500). M52/M52TU: cooling system failure (water pump, thermostat, £400–£1,000). N52: oil leaks — valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets (£500–£1,200) and valvetronic motor failure (£600–£1,200). Early M52: Nikasil bore wear (pre-1998).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 3 Series 328i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: 3 Series 328i values range from £2,000–£18,000 depending on generation, condition and mileage. A £2,200–£5,000 reconditioned engine represents 20–80% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained E90 or F30, replacement is often justified. For an ageing E36 with other issues, assess scrap value first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 3 Series 328i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 328i has used four different engines: M52B28 (1995–1999, 190bhp), M52TÜB28 (1997–2000, 193bhp), N52B30 (2006–2011, 230bhp), and N20B20 (2012–2015, 245bhp). Check your VIN or registration year. The code is stamped on the engine block.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. Timing chain and guide replacement costs £1,500–£3,500 and is significantly cheaper than a full engine replacement (£2,800–£5,500). BMW issued a recall on some N20 models. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — ignored chain wear can lead to catastrophic piston-valve contact.

eraMap.title: BMW 3 Series 328i — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 190hp six — Nikasil lining issue on early cars; cooling system weak
193hp — dual VANOS, aluminium block; cooling system is key watchpoint
230hp magnesium six — oil leaks and valvetronic motor are main issues
245hp turbo — timing chain guide failure is the defining risk

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current market ranges across all 328i generations. Older generations (E36/E46) typically run lower on the used market. See the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 3 Series 328i engine typically takes 8–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). N20 turbo adds complexity vs naturally aspirated sixes.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 3 Series 328i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential maintenance — ignore leads to bigger issues
Only viable on low-mileage, otherwise scrap and replace

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 3 Series 328i? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-1998 M52 (steel liners) or M52TU E46 — the most robust naturally aspirated sixes
* N52 E90 with documented oil leak history and valvetronic motor checked
* N20 F30 post-2015 (timing chain guides revised) or with chain replacement done
* Full service history with correct oil specification for the era
avoidIf: * Pre-1998 M52 with no history — Nikasil bore wear risk
* N20 F30 pre-2015 with no timing chain history — the risk is real
* Any 328i with poor service history — all engines are unforgiving of neglect
* N52 E90 with engine warning light and no diagnosis — valvetronic issues are expensive

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 328i badge spans 20 years of BMW engineering — from the classic M52 six to the modern N20 turbo. The E46 M52TU is the sweet spot for naturally aspirated reliability, the E90 N52 is a refined modern six, and the F30 N20 is the most powerful but the most risky. Buy on history, maintain the cooling system on the sixes and the timing chain on the turbo, and the 328i will reward you.

cta.label: → Compare BMW 3 Series 328i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 3 Series 328i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any rattle? N20 chain rattle is the biggest warning; M52/M52TU/N52 should be smooth
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — see the Era Map above
* Timing Chain History — N20: chains replaced? Tensioner checked? (pre-2015 is critical)
* Cooling System — M52/M52TU: any coolant loss? Water pump and thermostat history?
* Oil Leaks — N52: any burning smell or oil spots? Valve cover and oil filter housing are key
* Valvetronic — N52: any hesitation or engine light? Valvetronic motor is common
* Nikasil — E36 M52 pre-1998: check compression; high oil consumption is a warning
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
